free_text: 10B + alpha reactions at low energies
The Stable ion Accelerator for Nuclear
Astrophysics (Sta. ANA), the University of Notre Dame.
alpha-particle beams ranging in energy from
Ealpha-c.m. = 186 keV-1.43 MeV were produced with
intensities ranging from 0.5 to 120 microA on target.
Charged particles were detected using two 100
mm2 silicon surface barrier detectors (SSBDs), Mode
No.l Bu-013-100-100, placed 6.2 cm from the target at
90 and 135 degrees.
Uncertainties stem from the statistical and
common systematic uncertainty of the experimental data.
Author's comment: Nearly all of the figures labeled
"differential cross section" should really be labeled
something like "differential yield", since the thick
targets resulted in substantial distortion of the
observed yields from the true cross sections. Instead
of deconvoluting these effects from the data, we
instead applied an experimental response to the
R-matrix cross sections. The energies are also beam
energies and not effective energy.
Approved by R.J.deBoer on May 28, 2025.
BP
Target 1: Enriched 10B targets with thicknesses of
2.99e18 atoms/cm2 were produced by evaporation onto
0.5 mm thick tantalum backings, which also served as
a beam stop.
